action: Bridge boot handshake gap: cycle-274 smell-scan (codex-tmux) launched `codex --yolo -m gpt-5.5`, but codex 0.138.0 rendered an interactive update menu ('Update available! 0.138.0 -> 0.139.0 / 1. Update now / 2. Skip / Press enter to continue') before showing its REPL prompt. The bridge pasted the phase prompt anyway: the first Enter selected 'Update now' (codex quit to run `brew upgrade --cask codex` — upgrade did NOT take, Caskroom stayed 0.138.0, freeze held), and the remaining multi-line prompt spilled into the bare zsh shell, ending wedged at zsh `quote>` continuation. Phase produced zero deliverable for 25+ min while the observer extended on a liveness false-positive ('no fs growth for 10m0s but liveness probe active' — a wedged zsh PS2 prompt is NOT agent liveness). FatalPaneDetector (ADR-0044 S2) did not classify 'zsh: command not found: #' + shell-prompt-echo as fatal (EVOLVE_PHASE_RECOVERY=shadow records only). Fix direction, three layers: (a) boot-sync gate — bridge must verify the per-CLI REPL prompt marker / PaneBusy signature is present BEFORE injecting; the codex profile needs an update-menu signature ('Update available!' + 'Press enter to continue') handled by sending '2' (Skip) or launching with the update check suppressed (codex config/env, e.g. dismissed_version pre-write to ~/.codex/version.json which already exists from S3); (b) FatalPaneDetector signature for shell-prompt-echo ('zsh: command not found', PS2 'quote>' wedge, bare `user@host %` prompt after launch) so recovery fires instead of stalling; (c) observer stall policy must not count a dead shell prompt as liveness — liveness probe should require the launched CLI process (not the shell) alive in the pane. Acceptance: replay a recorded boot transcript containing the codex update menu through the bridge boot path in a test — injection must be withheld until prompt-marker confirmation, menu must be auto-dismissed via Skip, and a synthetic shell-spill pane must be classified fatal within one observer interval. RECURRENCE: same wedge hit cycle-274 architecture-design (second codex phase, 11:19Z, bquote> spill — transcript saved to .evolve/runs/cycle-274/architecture-design-boot-failure-scrollback.txt); EVERY codex boot wedges while the nag is pending, so this is batch-fatal, not one-off. Operator mitigation applied 11:25Z: wrote dismissed_version=0.139.0 into ~/.codex/version.json (equivalent of menu option 3 'Skip until next version') — durable fix must do this programmatically at preflight (S3 freeze step already owns ~/.codex/version.json) or gate injection on REPL prompt-marker.
evidence_pointer: .evolve/runs/cycle-274/smell-scan-observer-events.ndjson (stall_probe_active 11:00:08Z); tmux scrollback session evolve-bridge-codex-c274-smell-scan-pid43679-1781088603 (update menu + 'Updating Codex via brew upgrade' + zsh quote> wedge); ~/.codex/version.json latest_version=0.139.0 dismissed_version=0.137.0; brew Caskroom codex still 0.138.0
